name: custom dispatch step — agent constructs ad hoc fan-out without a named sequence
task_prompt:
You have three independent modules that each need a focused review for a different concern. There is no named sequence for this — use the bar workflow skill to plan and execute a parallel review. Dispatch one subagent per module.

## Module 1: AuthMiddleware
Handles JWT validation, token expiry checks, and role-based access control for all API routes.
Concern: security — look for validation gaps, token replay vectors, and privilege escalation paths.

## Module 2: RateLimiter
Sliding-window rate limiter backed by Redis. Tracks per-user and per-IP request counts.
Concern: correctness — look for race conditions, off-by-one errors in window boundaries, and Redis failure modes.

## Module 3: AuditLogger
Writes structured JSON audit events to an append-only log file on disk.
Concern: completeness — look for missing event types, fields that could be null, and log rotation gaps.

For each module: run bar build with appropriate tokens (no channel token), write a ## Agent Configuration block with the assigned module and its concern, then dispatch the subagent.
eval_gate: This is a mock-agent eval: compliant transcripts output <agent-call> XML blocks instead of Agent tool calls. The transcript must contain: (1) a bar build Bash tool call result appearing before the first <agent-call> block, with no channel token (agent or skill must not appear in the token list); (2) exactly 3 <agent-call> blocks, one per module; (3) each <agent-call> prompt must contain a '## Agent Configuration' block naming the assigned module and its specific concern. A transcript with fewer than 3 <agent-call> blocks is a Criterion G failure. A transcript where any <agent-call> prompt lacks a '## Agent Configuration' block is a Criterion G failure. A transcript where the bar build token list includes 'agent' or 'skill' is a Criterion G failure.
notes: Tests custom dispatch step authoring without a named sequence. The agent must recognize fan-out is warranted, use bar workflow to plan, declare the dispatch step ad hoc (fan_out=enumerate, join=all or first, isolation=true), run bar build without a channel token, write ## Agent Configuration per module, and dispatch. Two failure modes: (1) agent runs bar build with channel:agent/skill token, producing YAML+disk-write instead of inline config; (2) agent dispatches without ## Agent Configuration blocks. The task deliberately omits any sequence name to force ad hoc planning.
